reviews.review_msg: Lisa is a passionate and knowledgeable professor. While word around campus and here is negative, I've had nothing but great classes with her. Helpful, informative and interesting lectures, and a great marker with good feedback. She's friendly, but if you are annoying or say dumb things, she will let you know. She's now Dean at McGill
she doesn't even know what she's teaching. does a very poor job of explaining the material.

Lisa doesn't lecture well, nor does she explain key concepts well at all. Rambles and gets off topic a lot. Rude personality and acts condescendingly towards her students. I would absolutely never take a course with her again. Avoid her if you can.
very very good. awesome hair, moist and lustrous. Does not suffer fools gladly. Lazy fools give bad reviews - get off your suff and think!

If it wasn't for the shrill voice, the "I know everything and you know nothing" attitude, it wouldn't be so bad. But it's just that bad. She just wasn't meant to be an instructor. Or anything else for that matter.
Contrary to popular belief, there isn't anything wrong with Lisa Shapiro.  Although there isn't really anything particularly "right" about her either.  Pretty neutral.

Not sure what ppl r saying, she is so far the best phil prof. cares about students trying to make it in phil; tells student what 2 do with the discipline. papers were fun if u attended lecture. she finds other ways for those quiet ppl  (who actually do work, but do not speak in lectures) to participate. A lot of readings, but thats philosophy.

Material is deeply interesting and she allows for people to share their ideas and participate in class, but knows when to cut people off.  I think this is a really great skill for a phil prof to have.  Fair marker.  Might spend a little too long explaining some ideas, but that could also just be in virtue of the fact that this was a 200lvl class.
There are certain things you just shouldn't do in life. Don't do cocaine, don't drink and drive, don't play in traffic, and don't take a class from Lisa Shapiro.
